# Inflation-fingerprint grep over the repo (step-08 audit; .py + .md only).
# Pattern: _AV_SHARE_TARGETS|target_share|propulsion = total - av_total|\b10206\.97\b|\b19841\b|\b158730\b|\b39682\b|\b79365\b|\b18232\b|\b218784\b|\bA100\b|per[_ -]inference|inference frequency|per[_ -]agent inference|DRIVE AGX|Jetson|\bOrin\b|\bThor\b
# 'v3-v9 .../pages/02_Utility_Phase_Energy.py:_AV_SHARE_TARGETS' lines are
# the propulsion back-solve fixed in v10. 'one_time_data.py:18232/218784'
# are the manuscript reference constants v10 keeps for the comparison panel.
# Generated by scripts/audit_component_utility_v10.py

./v9_streamlit_app/pages/02_Utility_Phase_Energy.py:145:        target_share = _AV_SHARE_TARGETS[(fuel, lvl)]
./v9_streamlit_app/pages/02_Utility_Phase_Energy.py:146:        total = av_total / target_share
./v9_streamlit_app/pages/02_Utility_Phase_Energy.py:147:        propulsion = total - av_total

./audits/final_consistency/MASTER_NUMERICAL_RECONCILIATION.md:82:| L5 CAV (utility annual) | 18,232 | ECAV fleet-mean ≈ 1,500 (all cohorts) and 2,401 (ICECAV) | **UNVERIFIABLE from code** — the 18,232 figure is per-unit per-year for a single L5 at baseline, not the fleet-averaged value the dashboard reports. The `one_time_data.py::L5_UTILITY_ANNUAL_KWH = 18232.0` value is hard-coded for the inversion panel only; it is not re-derived from the simulator. |
./audits/final_consistency/PRIOR_DEFENSIBILITY_AUDIT.md:88:| Empirical anchor | NVIDIA Orin vs DRIVE Thor 4× performance-per-watt divergence. σ = 0.15 (±16 %) is tighter than the documented vendor-diversity range. **Flag**: σ = 0.15 is aggressive; σ = 0.25 would match NVIDIA generation divergence. |
./audits/final_consistency/CALCULATION_CORRECTNESS_AUDIT.md:36:computing energy as a per-inference cost multiplied by a
./audits/step_08_component_power_realignment/COMPONENT_REALIGNMENT_MEMO.md:16:computer < 100 W system; NVIDIA DRIVE Orin SoC 15–60 W; NVIDIA DRIVE Thor system;

./audits/step_08_component_power_realignment/COMPONENT_REALIGNMENT_MEMO.md:32:and computed `propulsion = av_total / target_share − av_total`. This forced the on-screen
./audits/step_08_component_power_realignment/COMPONENT_REALIGNMENT_MEMO.md:63:| Per-inference energy | Measured on **NVIDIA A100** (400 W TDP server GPU), Table 6 | Deployed automotive ASICs (Tesla FSD chip < 40 W; NVIDIA DRIVE Orin SoC 15–60 W; DRIVE Thor system) run the same INT8-quantised, pruned models at ~5–10× lower energy per inference and **batch all agents in one forward pass**. | The compute *slot* in the registry is sized by **deployed-silicon class** (Tesla-FSD-class at L3, Orin-class at L4, Thor-class at L5), not A100 energy. |
./audits/step_08_component_power_realignment/COMPONENT_REALIGNMENT_MEMO.md:66:| STI computing | Inherits the per-inference inflation × a 24/7 multiplier | Multiplies all of the above by ~8× duty | Bottom-up edge + HP-compute power at 24/7; ~0.8–1.0 kW continuous, not ~4.8–18 kW. |
./audits/step_08_component_power_realignment/COMPONENT_REALIGNMENT_MEMO.md:110:| Onboard Computing Unit — **L3** | Computing | 50 / 120 / 200 | vendor_conference | Tesla FSD HW3 board ~72–100 W (Hot Chips 2019); Mobileye EyeQ6 ~33 W/chip; NVIDIA Orin-N 15–40 W |
./audits/step_08_component_power_realignment/COMPONENT_REALIGNMENT_MEMO.md:111:| Onboard Computing Unit — **L4** | Computing | 100 / 220 / 350 | vendor_conference | NVIDIA DRIVE AGX Orin SoC 15–60 W (254 INT8 TOPS); deployed L4 platform w/ carrier+redundancy ~100–350 W |
./audits/step_08_component_power_realignment/COMPONENT_REALIGNMENT_MEMO.md:112:| Onboard Computing Unit — **L5** | Computing | 250 / 400 / 650 | vendor_conference | NVIDIA DRIVE Thor (~1000 INT8 TOPS / ~2000 FP4 TFLOPS); single→dual-Thor w/ full redundancy ~250–650 W |
./audits/step_08_component_power_realignment/COMPONENT_REALIGNMENT_MEMO.md:113:| Edge Computing Unit (STI) | Computing | 50 / 120 / 250 | vendor_estimate | NVIDIA Jetson AGX Orin module 15–60 W + carrier/networking/PoE in cabinet |
./audits/step_08_component_power_realignment/COMPONENT_REALIGNMENT_MEMO.md:114:| HP Computing Unit (STI) | Computing | 250 / 450 / 800 | vendor_estimate | DRIVE Thor-class accelerator / compact 2–4-GPU roadside edge server |
./audits/step_08_component_power_realignment/COMPONENT_REALIGNMENT_MEMO.md:141:Thor-class accelerators at ~250–650 W *and* the count doubles to 2 at L5 (Table 3). The
./audits/step_08_component_power_realignment/COMPONENT_REALIGNMENT_MEMO.md:178:(L3 ~120 W → L5 ~400 W) × a utilization rise (0.50→0.85), not from per-agent inference
./audits/step_08_component_power_realignment/COMPONENT_REALIGNMENT_MEMO.md:210:## 5. How the A100 / testbed data is now used
./audits/step_08_component_power_realignment/COMPONENT_REALIGNMENT_MEMO.md:212:The manuscript's Table 6 per-inference energies (4–62 J on the A100) **no longer feed
./audits/step_08_component_power_realignment/COMPONENT_REALIGNMENT_MEMO.md:262:   the A100-server-GPU benchmark, not of deployed silicon. The Table 5 absolute kWh should

./v10_streamlit_app/component_registry.py:12:in fielded CAVs (Tesla FSD HW3/HW4, NVIDIA DRIVE Orin/Thor, Waymo 5th-gen):
./v10_streamlit_app/component_registry.py:16:  1. Per-inference energy was measured on an NVIDIA A100 *server* GPU
./v10_streamlit_app/component_registry.py:17:     (paper Table 6) and used as the deployed per-inference cost. Deployed
./v10_streamlit_app/component_registry.py:18:     automotive ASICs (Tesla FSD chip <40 W, NVIDIA DRIVE Orin SoC 15–60 W,
./v10_streamlit_app/component_registry.py:19:     NVIDIA DRIVE Thor system) run the same workloads at far lower energy.
./v10_streamlit_app/component_registry.py:26:  4. STI inherited the same per-inference inflation × a 24/7 duty multiplier.
./v10_streamlit_app/component_registry.py:36:Tesla-FSD-class at L3, NVIDIA-DRIVE-Orin-class at L4, NVIDIA-DRIVE-Thor-class at
./v10_streamlit_app/component_registry.py:179:    # L3, NVIDIA-DRIVE-Orin-class at L4, NVIDIA-DRIVE-Thor-class at L5.
./v10_streamlit_app/component_registry.py:186:                                       # NVIDIA Orin-N 15-40 W. Deployed L3 platform
./v10_streamlit_app/component_registry.py:188:            "L4": _p(100, 220, 350),   # NVIDIA DRIVE AGX Orin SoC 15-60 W (254 INT8
./v10_streamlit_app/component_registry.py:191:            "L5": _p(250, 400, 650),   # NVIDIA DRIVE Thor (~1000 INT8 TOPS / ~2000
./v10_streamlit_app/component_registry.py:192:                                       # FP4 TFLOPS); single-Thor platform ~250-500 W,
./v10_streamlit_app/component_registry.py:193:                                       # dual-Thor with full redundancy up to ~1 kW.
./v10_streamlit_app/component_registry.py:200:            "per SoC. NVIDIA DRIVE AGX Orin: Jetson/DRIVE Orin SoC 15-60 W, 254 "
./v10_streamlit_app/component_registry.py:201:            "INT8 TOPS. NVIDIA DRIVE Thor (announced 2022): ~1000 INT8 TOPS / "

./v10_streamlit_app/V10_CHANGELOG.md:14:| Utility-phase per-unit energy | Flat `consumption_rates.ecav_power[L]` / `sti_power[L]` aggregates in `configs/<region>.json` — derived from NVIDIA-A100 server-GPU per-inference benchmarks × per-agent inference counts; the project config inflated these further (L5 CAV computing 19,841 kWh/yr → ~18 kW continuous on-vehicle compute). | Bottom-up `component_registry.py`: per-component **deployed automotive-silicon** power (Tesla FSD / NVIDIA DRIVE Orin / NVIDIA DRIVE Thor class for the compute slot at L3/L4/L5; vendor-spec sensors; V2X modules) × component counts (manuscript Extended Data Tables 3 & 4, re-used verbatim from `one_time_data.py`) × duty (~3 h/day CAV, 24 h/day STI) × per-level utilization × scenario factor. L5 CAV computing ≈ 745 kWh/yr (~0.68 kW). |
./v10_streamlit_app/V10_CHANGELOG.md:15:| Figure 4 propulsion bar | Back-solved: `propulsion = av_total / target_share − av_total` with a hard-coded `_AV_SHARE_TARGETS` table, forcing the on-screen autonomy share to match the manuscript. | The propulsion bar is the user-entered value, full stop. The autonomy share is whatever the bottom-up math produces — ~3.6 % (BEV L3) to ~22.7 % (BEV L5), ~1.5 % to ~10.6 % for ICE — i.e. the realistic 1–25 % range observed in fielded CAVs. |

./v10_streamlit_app/pages/02_Utility_Phase_Energy.py:407:  level (Tesla-FSD-class at L3 → NVIDIA-DRIVE-Orin-class at L4 →
./v10_streamlit_app/pages/02_Utility_Phase_Energy.py:408:  NVIDIA-DRIVE-Thor-class at L5) **and** the compute-unit count doubles at L5.

./v8_streamlit_app/pages/02_Utility_Phase_Energy.py:142:        total = av_total / target_share
